finding_results.notes: Rebuilt the decision block into four distinct beats: war-ending capability, concrete ward danger, social evidence that the war is already moving without Gavin, and the final choice. Removed the adjacent duplicate tradeoff summaries.

Made ward failure register through bodily and perceptual pressure on Gavin during the choice and subsequent work, and removed the explicit moral-certification line so the refusal stands without narrator absolution.

Distinguished fast local invalidation from slow global root editing. The chapter now establishes that a bounded battle-scale intervention could be done in minutes while the permanent everywhere rewrite still takes weeks.
Expanded the post-decision work loop and late-April castle deterioration with causally linked material. Final chapter word count is 5823 while preserving the heading contract.

Added three new Carrow speech acts to the corridor torture scene. Line 49: two-sentence block between curses — 'Told you about that, didn't I. Told you last week.' followed by 'You're carrying that round my corridor like I won't notice. You think I'm thick?' — establishing his repetitive scolding register and insecure-bully threat-question pattern with high contraction use and colloquial 'round my corridor.' Line 75: post-curse dismissal 'Pick it up and go' while already scanning the corridor, showing the banal transactional quality of his cruelty. Combined with the existing 'Get up' at line 71, the scene now carries four Carrow speech acts total. All new lines use contractions, contain imperatives or threat questions, and are unmistakably Carrow's blunt working-class register overlaid with petty authority — distinct from Gavin, Miren, and Babbling. No villain-monologue grandeur, no speechifying, no profanity beyond the level warranted by the scene's casual cruelty. The boy's inaudible response (line 51) and Carrow's wand-raise (line 53) provide action beats that keep the exchange from reading as transcript.

He would..." sequence was collapsed into a single sentence without changing the underlying decision.
finding_results.summary: Distinct local rewrites replaced the mechanical perception/anaphora template at the chapter's key discovery beats while preserving continuity, scene pressure, and Gavin's register.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised Chapter 15 in place to sharpen the war-versus-wards decision architecture, make ward failure physically intrusive instead of abstract, align local and global dark-magic intervention timing with later continuity, and extend the late-chapter deterioration/work sequence past the 5500-word floor.

Single finding addressed. Expanded Amycus Carrow's voiced presence in the corridor Cruciatus scene from one line ('Get up') to four speech acts spanning lines 49-75. Added a between-curses scolding block with high-contraction blunt register and a threat question ('You think I'm thick?'), plus a post-curse dismissal ('Pick it up and go'). All additions are local to the cited span (original lines 41-69), preserve surrounding prose and substrate-analysis passages unchanged, and maintain the scene's existing action choreography and Gavin's observational distance. No changes elsewhere in the chapter.
Resolved the prose/copyedit finding by varying the chapter's repeated revelation syntax and removing the exact "He could see" refrain from the targeted beats.
